transcript.text: To cover anyway, so I'll just break it.
This meeting is being recorded.
There we go.
Too many tabs.
Okay, there we go.
So one thing I want to call it is I see that there is a few articles that you left comments on that we have not closed out, which we are going to do after this.
They kind of slipped through the cracks.
So we did get those all moved through today.
And then there are five more articles in production.
Some of it will come to you today and probably some tomorrow, but all by the end of this week.
Okay, sounds good.
And we've got a good runway of approved topics right now.
So we're kind of all good there.
Unless there's anything you want to flag for us.
No, I don't think so.
I think we're looking good.
Yeah, and so I have updated sort of the artifacts in the pipeline based on the kind of features, I guess not features matrix, but.
The sort of features detail that you sent.
So that's all updated.
And I'm hoping that that's going to kind of like catch some of the issues we were having where it was going a little too far in recommending features or like creating features that don't exist.
So we've sort of set that to really use that as the source of truth.
So I'm going to continue to like keep an eye on how it's treating that.
But I'm hoping that I'll kind of be the answer to that and also let it give a little more detail.
Great.
Yeah, that was a good thing to set up.
Yeah, definitely.
So another one, actually one other thing I wanted to talk about is we've seen across some other client accounts that have been experimenting with this.
Yeah.
Adding an FAQ to the, and there's sort of two things.
There's like the FAQ and then there's the schema, which is like what you would add to those sort of the blog posts on your end.
And we've seen that both of those seem to have, offer a lift in terms of LLM traffic.
So I think one of the things that I might recommend if you're opening, if you're open to it is sort of just adding a short FAQ section at the bottom of blog posts as just standard practice.
That's something we can build into the pipeline.
And either across the board or, you know, if you don't like that idea, we can also like experiment and do it on some of them.
But we have seen it have an impact on LLM traffic.
So I think it's worth considering.
Yeah, no, let's do it.
Would it be the same like shared FAQ across all the articles or it would be specific to each article?
It would be specific to each article.
Okay, and so you're saying that would be set up on my end?
So we would do the FAQ content, the schema.
is something that's a little bit different.
That's something added to the template of the blog post, like on your end, like the setup of code.
But it would be, sorry, keep on.
I was going to say, so it's usually something that you only have to update once, is my understanding.
So I can get more details because the technical part of it is not my area of expertise.
But it should be something that you think you can add once on the backend and then get produced across.
If that's the case, yeah, that was going to be my next question.
Yeah, if that's the case, let's give it a shot.

Yeah, and if it's not something, if it's something you have questions about doing on your end, I can like back down more specific, like how that works in Webflow, because everything is a little bit different.
And in terms of schema, like it's something that you can add to all the core pages on your website.
And I know that other people, both our clients and also.

Just based on what people are talking about on LinkedIn in terms of seeing traffic and other companies that have really leaned into LLM visibility, they're seeing an uptick in traffic when they add schema to the core pages of their website.
Okay.
So yeah, this is interesting because so before I joined Otto, they had hired a marketing contractor part-time to make some updates to the Webflow website.
Right.
And I noticed that he had set up some, I guess you call them FAQ type schema pages in the CMS system.
And I don't think we've linked to those pages.
And I was kind of confused about exactly why he did that and how it works.
But that's in my mind too.
Like as we approach this, like I should look at all that and make sure that's what we want.
And, you know, is that making sense?
So yeah, I think it's something to think about.
I know.
I know, like Webflow just publicly released.

I think they did some experimentation around this, adding this kind of schema markup to both their content and also like their blog content and also just the core pages of their website and said that they did tie it to like a big lift in LLM visibility.
And it's one of those things, too, where it can't hurt, right?
Like it just makes it's like search, right?
It just makes your page easier for them to even understand what's going on.

So I feel like these things where it feels like it's not even going to impact, you know, the experience from a user perspective, it can only help in terms of both search and LLM visibility feels like a bit of a like, why not?
Yeah, perfect.
And so, yeah, once I wrap my head around all this, I'll make sure to do it for our homepage, too, because, you know, that's like 90% of the pages that I own that actually, you know, that matter.
So if I can do it on the page to start with, at least.
Because we actually do have a small FAQ type, like that's user facing, like, you know, five or six questions.
But yeah, if we can get something more behind the scenes going to that, that should be good.
Yeah.
And it's something where, you know, I would like for us at GrowthX to, you know, pull together, it'd be great to pull together sort of a best practices based on what we're seeing across accounts.
And so I'm not sure that exists yet.
I think this is just, like, more of a conversation that's happening this week around this, but something I just wanted to flag.
Because there's certain things we can do on our end, and then obviously there's other stuff you can do on your end.
Okay.
Well, maybe before we meet next time, I'll try to look into all that, and then I'll just come back with questions, and we can kind of talk in more in depth when I've taken a look.
yeah, that sounds good.
And I'll see if I gather anything else that feels like good context, I can also drop it in Slack.
Okay, great.
Oh, I want to touch base next week.
I will be off.
On Thursday for Thanksgiving.
Are you okay with doing an async or we could move it, try to move our meeting until Wednesday?
Let's just do an async.
We've got a bunch of people out.
I don't really know what I'm doing.
So yeah.
Yes.
Okay.
That sounds good.
So I'll just plan to send out async updates on Wednesday then.
Okay.
Great.
So in terms of reporting, we did update the cohorts in your looker now.
So they'll match the blog clusters, which I think will be useful going forward to kind of still be able to see, you know, what's performing really well.
What's not.
So, oh, and I also wanted to flag.
So we are planning, Jacob's planning to do sort of a deep dive reporting on your account that week of December 8th.
Just so you sort of have that on your calendar.
And if you have any kind of specific questions around what you're seeing or things you'd specifically like to see, you know, you can let us know or drop that in Slack.
And he can kind of speak to, I think he'll speak generally to everything, but.
If you have like specific concerns or questions moving forward, it'd be a time to raise them ahead of that.

So in terms of just like signals we're seeing this week, you know, that number we like it seen going up into the right, you know, so growth in traffic, especially, you know, we've seen pretty strong growth over the last week.
As always, like anything week over week, you have like, sometimes it's hard to tell what's signal and what's noise, you know, over that kind of timeframe, but still good to see.
Same thing with search momentum, you know, the number of clicks coming from organic content is still small.
But again, that sort of like early signals is like good to see it's all trending in the right direction.
In terms of LLM, traffic, same thing, know, the, it's not, the numbers aren't huge, but it's growing and the, the.
Signals are positive.
The engagement is really high on LLM referral traffic, which is, you know, good to see.

And I also, one thing I noticed comparing the Scrunch dashboard with kind of the Looker, which is all the Google Search Console, is that the sort of like top versus middle versus bottom positions has seemed to make a difference in terms of like the actual click rate.

So sometimes with these, it's hard to tell how much of a difference these things make, but it, in terms of engagement, it did like staying in that top position, moved that average click rate to like 58% versus 40%.
So it's interesting to know.
And then one big growth was then the like, in terms of the actual ownership of citation is still small, but it grew, you know, 70% in the last week.
So again, you know, small numbers, but like all trending in the direction that we like, we like.
So it'll be exciting to see more months and hopefully the numbers will be even more exciting.
Yeah, I should let you know too, we were anticipating our general release happening this week actually, but we pushed that back two weeks.
I'm not sure if we've told you that we're expecting to have much more direct traffic and probably some press and all that kind of stuff.
But that'll be happening, you know, early to mid-December.
Okay, that's good to know.
We'll make sure that's sort of on our calendar.
But that's always fun to see the spike in traffic that comes.
Yeah.

And it's funny and we've also seen, you know, it's also interesting to see too, because that sort of spike in traffic around a launch, you get to see when it comes through search versus like when it comes through LLMs.
And it serves as kind of an interesting experiment to kind of understand the time.
And how that stuff tends to trickle in.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And I do anticipate it being more of a trickle.
Like, I don't think there's going to be like a particular date where it's, you know, it's everything and then it just falls off.
I think it'll probably be rolling a bit with some different, hopefully some different press articles coming out.
And I think in terms of that, like the press stuff, I think for LLMs is like hugely important because you get those third party citations.
And so sometimes we've seen, you know, big lifts around that sort of outsized lift.
Yeah, totally.

And so one of the things that's on my list to do in the next week or so is add a press page where I'm going to be, you know, every time there's a new article or something, I'll set up a, you know, photo and a link to it and all that kind of stuff.
And I'll do that in the CMS so that we kind of capture all that stuff and have it on our site.
Yeah, would actually be interesting.
I wonder, like, if you get some really...
Good press.
Like, there's probably a way to, like, I don't know, if you end up in tool roundups and stuff, like, it would be good to flag those links for our system as links to drive to as well.
And, like, it should be the research stage of the pipeline should do that, but it would be good to sort of be aware of those to make sure you're doing that.
Okay, yeah, I'll flag that and, you know, keep track of those as I see them.
Yeah, and I imagine, like, you'll be adding them to your website, too, so we can always check there.
But I'll be curious just to make, you know, see if it does a good job with that and kind of be on that on our end to make sure it does.
Okay.
All right, I think that is it for me today.
Unless you have anything else you want to chat about?
No, I don't.
So, yeah, have a good vacation or time off or whatever you're doing.
yeah, for you, happy, yeah, Thanksgiving, we'll be doing the turkey and everything.
Nice.
Nice.
Yeah, I think we are too.
We're just staying home though.
I know.
Well, it's kind of nice when you don't have to travel actually.
Sometimes those are like the best holidays.
No traffic.
think Thanksgiving is supposed to be like the worst traffic holiday.
Totally.
Yeah.
It's so like condensed, like all the travel is happening on the same time.
All right.
Well, sounds good.
Well, yeah.
In the meantime, feel free to reach out on Slack if anything comes up.
Okay.
Sounds good.
All right.
Okay.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content pipeline, new SEO tactics, and performance signals.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New SEO Tactic:** Add per-article FAQs and site-wide FAQ schema to boost LLM visibility, a strategy proven effective on other client accounts.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=96.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** The content pipeline now uses a feature matrix as its source of truth to prevent generating non-existent features. Five new articles are in production.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=50.0)
  - [**Strong Performance Signals:** Key metrics are trending positively, including high LLM traffic engagement and a 70% WoW increase in citation ownership.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
  - [**Launch Rescheduled:** The general release is pushed back two weeks to early-mid December, creating a new opportunity to analyze traffic sources (search vs. LLM) from the expected press coverage.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Quality

  - [All outstanding article comments are now resolved.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=11.0)
  - [Five new articles are in production, with delivery expected by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=23.0)
  - [The pipeline now uses the feature matrix as its source of truth.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=50.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To prevent generating non-existent features and ensure greater detail accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=50.0)
      - [**Status:** GrowthX will monitor its effectiveness.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)

### New SEO Tactic: FAQs & Schema

  - [**Proposal:** Add per-article FAQs and site-wide FAQ schema to boost LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=96.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This strategy has driven significant traffic lifts on other GrowthX client accounts.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=96.0)
  - [**Implementation Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
      - [**FAQ Content:** GrowthX will generate the per-article FAQ content.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
      - [**FAQ Schema:** Jason will implement the technical schema markup in Webflow.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
          - [**Rationale:** A one-time backend update is expected to apply the schema site-wide.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=195.0)
          - [**Action:** Jason will investigate existing schema setup from a previous contractor.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
      - [**Scope:** Apply schema to all core pages, including the homepage.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=229.0)

### Performance & Reporting

  - [**Looker Dashboard:** Cohorts are now updated to match blog clusters, enabling more granular performance analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=438.0)
  - [**Deep Dive:** Jacob (GrowthX) will conduct a deep dive into account reporting during the week of Dec 8.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
      - [**Action:** Jason to provide specific questions or areas of concern via Slack before the deep dive.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
  - [**Key Performance Signals:**](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Traffic:** Strong WoW growth across all sources.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Search:** Positive momentum; organic clicks are small but growing.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=513.0)
      - [**LLM Traffic:** Growing with high engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=528.0)
          - [**CTR Impact:** Top search position → 58% CTR vs. 40% for middle positions.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
      - [**Citation Ownership:** Grew 70% WoW (from a small base).](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=583.0)

### General Release & Press

  - [**Launch Rescheduled:** General release moved from this week to early-mid December.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
      - [**Impact:** Expect a traffic spike from press coverage and direct marketing.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
      - [**Opportunity:** Analyze traffic sources (search vs. LLM) during the launch.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
  - [**Press Page:** Jason will create a press page in the Webflow CMS to centralize all coverage.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=695.0)
      - [**Action:** Jason to flag high-value press links (e.g., tool roundups) for the GrowthX pipeline to target.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=715.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jason:**](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
      - [Investigate existing FAQ schema setup in Webflow.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
      - [Implement new FAQ schema site-wide.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
      - [Create a press page in Webflow CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=695.0)
      - [Flag high-value press links for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=715.0)
      - [Submit questions for the Dec 8 reporting deep dive via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
      - [Generate per-article FAQ content for the pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
      - [Send async update on Wednesday, Nov 26.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=415.0)
      - [Conduct reporting deep dive during the week of Dec 8.](https://fathom.video/share/J3zwZ_3xkaxsit4-quJyKW-QyakfyqHB?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)

action_items.description: Add per-article FAQ to all new blog posts
Research Webflow FAQ/schema; then implement FAQPage schema sitewide incl homepage
Send async update to Jason on Nov 26
Add Dec 8 deep-dive reporting to calendar
Add early–mid Dec general release to calendar
Flag press links to Bailey for pipeline targeting
